title: campomanesia dichotoma
intro: Campomanesia dichotoma, also known as the white guava or white champakan, is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. The plant is native to Brazil and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Campomanesia dichotoma include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and skin conditions.
